summary: Iteration 3 delta testing (Catálogo rename, real photo in Hero + Customizer, live color tint of real shoe photo). Backend: 6/6 pytest cases still passing (health, contact, designs with color_acento, validation). Frontend: 19/21 checks passed. Confirmed: Navbar label renamed to 'Catálogo' (data-testid still nav-productos) and its click smooth-scrolls to #productos; Hero and Customizer both use the real product photo (0f5dh8f6_Image.png); accent 'Franjas' chip live-updates when opt-acento-* is picked; personaje badge SVG appears; nombre-nino ribbon appears; empty-required submit shows error toast; valid submit persists to /api/designs with color_acento and shows success toast + confetti; catalog renders all 5 real products with Personalizar buttons; contact form persists. BUG FOUND: the main color-principal tint layer in ShoePreview.jsx does NOT unmount the initial (default Rosa Chicle #FF3366) overlay when the user picks another color — subsequent picks correctly swap among themselves, but the very first Rosa overlay stays in the DOM stacked with mix-blend-mode: multiply on top of the newly selected color. Visual effect: after picking Turquesa the shoe looks purple/blue (Rosa*Turquesa), after Verde Menta the shoe looks muddy greenish-grey, etc.

ui_bugs.issue: Initial color-principal <motion.div> overlay is never unmounted after color change. Two multiply overlays stack (default Rosa Chicle rgb(255,51,102) + selected color), producing wrong final tint. DOM verified: container children count goes from 5 -> 6 after first color change and stays at 6 for every subsequent change (the orphan Rosa layer persists). Screenshot shows Turquesa selected but shoe appears purple.

action_items: Fix ShoePreview.jsx color-principal tint layer: replace the `key={colorPrincipal}` motion.div pattern with either (a) a single stable motion.div using `animate={{ backgroundColor: colorPrincipal }}`, or (b) wrap it in <AnimatePresence mode="wait"> so the previous overlay is properly unmounted before the new one mounts. Currently the first-mounted Rosa Chicle overlay persists in the DOM and multiplies with every subsequent selection, producing incorrect final tints.
critical_code_review_comments: ShoePreview.jsx line 29-38: key-change on motion.div without AnimatePresence is not reliably unmounting; use AnimatePresence mode='wait' or animate background via `animate` prop to avoid stacked overlays.
server.py remains small and clean; no _id leaks (projections use {_id:0}). No changes needed.
Consider adding an accessible alt text or aria-label difference between hero and customizer since both use the same PREVIEW_IMG/HERO_IMG URL.
seed_data_creation: Added TEST_-prefixed rows via UI + pytest: 1 design (TEST_QA Iter3 Cliente, qa_iter3@test.com), 1 contact (qa_iter3_contact@test.com), plus 2 pytest-generated designs and 1 pytest-generated contact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is green (6/6). The only outstanding issue is a visual bug in the customizer live-preview color tint: the initial Rosa Chicle overlay in ShoePreview.jsx is never unmounted, so it stacks (mix-blend-mode: multiply) with every subsequent color pick. DOM inspection confirms container children count 5 -> 6 on first change and stays at 6. To retest the fix: open #disena, verify DOM under [data-testid='customizer-preview'] img.parentElement has exactly ONE div with mix-blend-mode: multiply after selecting any color; and visually the shoe should clearly take the selected hue (Turquesa should look turquoise, not purple).
rca of the issue: Root cause: In /app/frontend/src/components/ShoePreview.jsx (lines 29-38), the main color tint is rendered as `<motion.div key={colorPrincipal} initial={{opacity:0}} animate={{opacity:0.62}} ... />` conditionally on !isWhite(colorPrincipal). When the user changes color, React sees the same conditional (still !isWhite) with a new key; it should unmount the old element and mount a new one. However, framer-motion's active animation lifecycle appears to keep the previously-mounted element alive (no AnimatePresence to orchestrate exit). Empirically, only the FIRST mount is orphaned — subsequent key swaps correctly replace each other. Reproduction: (1) load /#disena, (2) inspect img.parentElement.children.length -> 5, (3) click any opt-color-* other than default -> length becomes 6 with both rgb(255,51,102) and the new color as siblings, both with mix-blend-mode multiply, both opacity 0.62, (4) click a third color -> length stays 6, the second overlay swaps but the first Rosa Chicle overlay remains. Mitigation: wrap the conditional motion.div in `<AnimatePresence mode="wait">` and re-run — or simplify to a single motion.div and animate `backgroundColor` via the `animate` prop (no key).
